reasoning: The North Pacific jet stream produces well-documented eastbound flight-time advantages (e.g., Tokyo→LA ~2h shorter than LA→Tokyo). This asymmetry has been measured continuously since commercial transpacific aviation began in the 1950s. The dome relabels this as a "Northern Pacific return current" in its aetheric framework, but the phenomenon is fully explained by the subtropical jet stream — a consequence of the Coriolis effect on a rotating sphere, angular momentum conservation, and meridional temperature gradients. The prediction was registered 2026-03-28 but the data has been public for 70+ years, making it post-hoc by definition. By the author's own "What Prospective Means" standard, this would be classified as backtested.
